text:
HEMATOLOGY-ONCOLOGY OUTPATIENT CLINIC NOTE
Post-Induction Day +35 Assessment

ATTENDING: Hematology/Oncology
VISIT TYPE: Outpatient follow-up

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
Day +35 post-induction bone marrow assessment follow-up. Patient presenting to discuss marrow results and plan for further therapy.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Ms. Vasquez-Okonkwo is a 48-year-old female biology teacher with ELN adverse-risk acute myeloid leukemia -- specifically FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio (AR 0.91), NPM1 wild-type, with trisomy 8 cytogenetics and a complex co-mutation profile including DNMT3A R882H, TET2 Q1299*, WT1 R462W, NRAS Q61H, KDM6A, STAG2, and BCOR mutations identified on comprehensive 468-gene NGS panel at diagnosis. She presented approximately 6 weeks ago with WBC 87,400/uL, hemoglobin 7.2 g/dL, platelets 28,000/uL, 42% peripheral blasts, and 74% bone marrow blasts by aspirate differential -- consistent with M1-type AML by FAB classification.

She was admitted to the leukemia service and initiated on induction chemotherapy per the RATIFY protocol design: cytarabine 100 mg/m2/day continuous infusion days 1-7 plus daunorubicin 60 mg/m2 IV days 1, 2, and 3 (7+3), with midostaurin 50 mg PO BID on days 8-21. Her induction course was complicated by: E. coli bacteremia (day 10-18, blood cultures from one bottle, managed with escalation from ciprofloxacin to cefepime; defervesced within 48 hours), Grade 2 mucositis requiring opioid analgesia (resolved by day 21), and expected transfusion dependence (2 units pRBCs on day 14; 3 platelet transfusions during nadir). No tumor lysis syndrome. No significant hepatotoxicity. QTc remained acceptable on midostaurin throughout (max 438 ms).

Day +35 bone marrow biopsy results are now available and were reviewed by the attending hematopathologist. The marrow is hypocellular (10-15% cellularity) with trilineage hematopoiesis and morphologically estimated myeloblasts less than 3%. Multiparameter flow cytometry MRD (10-color panel, sensitivity 0.01%) is NEGATIVE -- no aberrant blast immunophenotype detected. FLT3-ITD PCR on bone marrow is not detected (below 10^-4 sensitivity threshold). WT1 quantitative PCR is 26 copies/10^4 ABL1 copies, within the normal range. Today's peripheral CBC shows WBC 3,200/uL with ANC 1,850/uL, hemoglobin 9.8 g/dL, and platelets 112,000/uL -- consistent with marrow recovery following induction aplasia. These results collectively meet ELN 2022 criteria for complete remission (CR), and this is an MRD-negative CR after a single induction course -- a very encouraging early response in a patient with high-risk disease.

She presents today to review these results in detail and to discuss next steps, specifically the question of consolidation chemotherapy versus proceeding directly to allogeneic stem cell transplantation. She is accompanied by her husband.

She reports that over the past two weeks she has noticed a meaningful improvement in energy. She is eating a soft diet and tolerating it without nausea. She has resumed most activities of daily living and is ambulating independently at home. She has not had any fevers, chills, or new bleeding since discharge. She denies chest pain, dyspnea at rest, or lower extremity edema. She notes mild residual mouth soreness but no active ulcerations. She denies any new rashes or skin changes.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Constitutional: No fever, no chills, no night sweats. Improved appetite. Mild fatigue, improving compared to hospitalization nadir.
HEENT: Mild residual oral soreness, no active ulcerations. No gingival bleeding since prior admission (resolved pre-induction). No visual changes.
Respiratory: No dyspnea at rest; mild exertional dyspnea with prolonged stair climbing, improved from presentation.
Cardiovascular: No chest pain, palpitations, or lower extremity edema.
Gastrointestinal: Tolerating soft diet. No nausea or vomiting at home. Bowel function normalizing; no significant diarrhea.
Genitourinary: No dysuria or hematuria.
Musculoskeletal: Generalized mild weakness and deconditioning from hospitalization. No bone pain.
Neurologic: No headache, dizziness, or focal neurologic deficits.
Skin: No rashes, no petechiae, no ecchymoses.
Endocrine: Continues levothyroxine for hypothyroidism; no symptoms of hypo- or hyperthyroidism. Blood pressure controlled at home.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
1. Hypothyroidism -- on levothyroxine, well-controlled
2. Mild hypertension -- managed medically, well-controlled
3. Acute myeloid leukemia (AML), NOS, FAB M1 -- diagnosed approximately 6 weeks prior (see HPI); ELN 2022 adverse risk; FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio (AR 0.91), NPM1 wild-type, trisomy 8; currently in MRD-negative CR1 following 7+3 + midostaurin induction

SOCIAL HISTORY:
High school biology teacher; currently on medical leave from work. Married, two adult children; strong support system at home. Non-smoker, no tobacco history. Occasional social alcohol (1-2 drinks per week, none during treatment). No illicit drug use. No significant environmental or occupational chemical exposures. Of mixed Afro-Caribbean and Eastern European descent. Lives locally, able to attend clinic regularly.

FAMILY HISTORY:
Not fully characterized in prior notes. No known history of hematologic malignancy in first-degree relatives. Mother with breast cancer (age at diagnosis not specified). Father with coronary artery disease.

ALLERGIES:
No known drug allergies documented at this time. Tolerated cefepime, ciprofloxacin, ondansetron, prochlorperazine, opioids without documented allergic reactions during induction hospitalization.

MEDICATIONS (current at time of this visit):
1. Levothyroxine 75 mcg PO daily (hypothyroidism)
2. Amlodipine 5 mg PO daily (hypertension -- or equivalent antihypertensive per prior outpatient regimen)
3. Allopurinol 300 mg PO daily (tumor lysis prophylaxis -- to be reassessed; may discontinue given CR)
4. Fluconazole 400 mg PO daily (antifungal prophylaxis, per post-induction protocol)
5. Acyclovir 400 mg PO BID (viral prophylaxis)
6. Ciprofloxacin 500 mg PO daily (antibacterial prophylaxis -- transitional)
7. Ondansetron 8 mg PO q8h PRN nausea
8. Multivitamin

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vital Signs: Temperature 37.0°C, blood pressure 118/74 mmHg, heart rate 82 bpm, respiratory rate 16/min, SpO2 98% on room air. Weight 67.3 kg (down from admission weight of ~72 kg, 4.7 kg weight loss during induction hospitalization).
General: Well-appearing, alert and oriented x3. No acute distress. Appropriately dressed. Mild appearance of fatigue.
Head/Eyes/Ears/Nose/Throat: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Conjunctivae mildly pale but improving; no scleral icterus. Oropharynx: healing oral mucosa, no active ulcerations, no thrush, minimal residual erythema of soft palate. No active gingival bleeding. Good dentition.
Neck: Supple. No cervical, supraclavicular, or anterior/posterior cervical lymphadenopathy appreciated.
Respiratory: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No crackles, wheezes, or rhonchi.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. Normal S1 and S2. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No peripheral edema.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. Bowel sounds active in all quadrants. Liver edge palpable approximately 1 cm below right costal margin -- trace hepatomegaly, markedly improved from prior. SPLENOMEGALY RESOLVED -- spleen no longer palpable on deep inspiration or manual palpation. No ascites appreciated.
Skin: No petechiae, purpura, or ecchymoses. No rash. Skin intact. Central line site (if retained) without signs of infection.
Extremities: No lower extremity edema. No calf tenderness.
Neurologic: Alert and oriented. Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Motor strength 4+/5 throughout (mild diffuse deconditioning). No focal deficits.
Lymphatic: No axillary, inguinal, or other peripheral lymphadenopathy.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS:
CBC (today): WBC 3,200/uL; ANC 1,850/uL; hemoglobin 9.8 g/dL; MCV 88 fL; platelets 112,000/uL
Differential: neutrophils 58%, lymphocytes 32%, monocytes 7%, eosinophils 2%, basophils 1% -- no blasts seen on manual differential
CMP: Sodium 138, potassium 4.0, chloride 103, bicarbonate 26, BUN 12, creatinine 0.78 mg/dL, glucose 94. Total bilirubin 0.6 mg/dL, AST 28 U/L, ALT 31 U/L, alkaline phosphatase 72 U/L, total protein 6.4 g/dL, albumin 3.7 g/dL
LDH: 178 U/L (upper limit of normal per our lab approximately 240 U/L)
Magnesium: 1.9 mg/dL
Uric acid: 4.2 mg/dL
TSH: 2.1 mIU/L (within normal limits on current levothyroxine)
Coagulation: PT 12.4 sec, INR 1.0, PTT 28 sec -- normalized (previously elevated at presentation)

Bone Marrow Biopsy/Aspirate (Day +35, reviewed today):
- Cellularity 10-15%, hypocellular but regenerating; trilineage hematopoiesis present
- Morphology: myeloblasts less than 3%; no Auer rods identified; no abnormal blast clusters
- Flow MRD (10-color): NEGATIVE (no aberrant CD34+/CD117+/CD13+/CD33+/CD123+/MPO+ blast population detected above 0.01% sensitivity threshold)
- FLT3-ITD PCR (bone marrow): NOT DETECTED
- WT1 qPCR: 26 copies/10^4 ABL1 (normal reference less than 50)
- Conventional cytogenetics pending (expected to show normal or recovering marrow; trisomy 8 clone should be absent in CR)

HLA typing (sent at diagnosis): Results available -- see transplant referral section below

---

IMAGING:
No new imaging performed at this visit. Prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis at presentation showed splenomegaly 16.4 cm, mild hepatomegaly, no lymphadenopathy, no extramedullary disease. Splenomegaly clinically resolved on today's exam. Pre-transplant CT will be ordered as part of workup.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

Ms. Vasquez-Okonkwo is a 48-year-old female with newly diagnosed ELN adverse-risk AML -- FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio (0.91), NPM1 wild-type, trisomy 8, with DNMT3A R882H founder mutation and multiple co-mutations on NGS -- who has achieved a complete remission with MRD negativity after a single course of 7+3 plus midostaurin induction chemotherapy. This is an excellent early result, though her underlying disease biology remains high-risk and the probability of relapse without definitive consolidation is substantial.

1. AML IN CR1, MRD-NEGATIVE -- PROCEEDING TO ALLOGENEIC STEM CELL TRANSPLANTATION:

The day +35 marrow assessment confirms CR by all criteria: morphologic CR (blasts less than 5%), hematologic recovery (ANC greater than 1,000/uL, platelets greater than 100,000/uL), MRD negativity by flow cytometry and FLT3-ITD PCR, and WT1 transcript within normal range. This is an ideal outcome after first induction.

However, the multidisciplinary leukemia team -- including hematology/oncology, transplant, and pathology -- has discussed this case in our weekly tumor board. There is strong consensus that this patient requires consolidation with allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (allo-HSCT) in CR1. The rationale is compelling:
- FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio without NPM1 co-mutation confers a relapse rate in excess of 50-60% with chemotherapy alone even after MRD-negative CR1
- DNMT3A R882H co-mutation further worsens prognosis in this context and is associated with chemotherapy resistance and relapse
- Trisomy 8 and the complex co-mutation landscape (TET2, WT1, NRAS, STAG2, BCOR) add additional adverse prognostic weight
- She is 48 years old with an ECOG PS of 1, HCT-CI score of 1 (hypothyroidism), and no significant organ dysfunction -- she is an excellent transplant candidate
- The graft-versus-leukemia (GVL) effect from allo-HSCT offers the best available chance of durable disease control in this setting

HLA typing was sent at diagnosis. Results are now available. She is typed at 10 alleles (HLA-A, B, C, DRB1, DQB1). We have reviewed these results with the transplant team. A bone marrow registry search has been initiated through the Be The Match (NMDP) registry. She has been formally referred to our stem cell transplant service for consultation and pre-transplant evaluation.

We discussed the rationale for transplant at length with the patient and her husband today. She understands the risks and benefits of allo-HSCT, including graft-versus-host disease, infectious complications, engraftment failure, transplant-related mortality, and the risk of relapse post-transplant despite transplant. She was counseled that proceeding to transplant in CR1 with MRD negativity represents the most favorable time to undergo this procedure, and that waiting for relapse would substantially worsen outcomes. She expressed willingness to proceed.

No additional consolidation chemotherapy cycles are planned prior to transplantation. The decision to proceed directly from CR1 to transplant without interim HiDAC consolidation is supported by recent data and is consistent with our institutional approach for fit patients with an identified donor and adverse-risk AML -- avoiding additional toxicity while minimizing time off the marrow nadir.

2. POST-INDUCTION SUPPORTIVE CARE AND MONITORING:
- Continue antimicrobial prophylaxis (fluconazole, acyclovir, antibacterial) per protocol; will reassess as counts recover
- Allopurinol can be tapered and discontinued given confirmed CR and adequate renal function
- Continue levothyroxine and antihypertensive as per usual dosing
- Recommend low-impact physical activity and nutritional rehabilitation as tolerated
- Monitor CBC weekly until counts fully recover and remain stable
- Follow-up CBC and CMP in 1-2 weeks

3. PRE-TRANSPLANT WORKUP (to be coordinated with transplant team):
- CT chest/abdomen/pelvis (staging/baseline pre-transplant)
- Echocardiogram (LVEF assessment for conditioning)
- Pulmonary function tests (FEV1, DLCO for busulfan-based conditioning eligibility)
- CMV serostatus (patient; already known from induction workup)
- Dental evaluation
- Fertility consultation -- offered to patient given age 48 and upcoming conditioning-related gonadotoxicity; patient deferred given post-menopausal status (confirmed, age-appropriate)
- Infectious disease evaluation if indicated
- Dental clearance

4. PSYCHOSOCIAL:
The patient and her husband are processing the diagnosis and the trajectory ahead. She expressed some anxiety about transplant and its potential complications but demonstrates excellent understanding and engagement with the plan. Social work referral placed to assist with emotional support, logistics of transplant hospitalization, and coordination of medical leave from work. Patient education materials about allo-HSCT provided. Contact information for our transplant nurse coordinator given.

5. RETURN VISIT:
She will be seen in the transplant clinic within the next 2-3 weeks for formal transplant consultation. She will follow up in our leukemia clinic concurrently. Laboratory work to be checked in 1-2 weeks at her local lab with results faxed to our office.

All questions were addressed. Patient verbalized understanding of the plan. Time spent on this visit including counseling: approximately 60 minutes.

Electronically signed,
Attending Hematologist-Oncologist
Hematologic Malignancies Program
